fleet_size.fact: Robotaxi fleet tracker showed ~519-522 active vehicles, with ~8 unsupervised in Austin, representing ~8-9% weekly growth — the largest single-week growth in the program's history
Robotaxi fleet tracker: 474 total vehicles, including 3 Cybercabs listed as driverless/unsupervised
Public Robo Tracker showed 301 total vehicles — 213 in Bay Area and 88 in Austin — with 7 operating fully unsupervised in Austin. Tesla's own Q4 earnings call referenced approximately 500 vehicles
Tesla already has approximately 90 to 100 vehicles operating without anyone behind the wheel
Cybercab production ramp estimate: ~100 units in April, hundreds/month in May, ~1,000/month in June; a few thousand total in Q2 2026

Over 100 Cybercabs had already been built and shipped out of Giga Texas by mid-March 2026. Projected ramp: 1,000+/month by May, 2,000/month by June-July, 5,000/month by Q3/early Q4. Full-year 2026 estimate: 20,000-40,000 units
15-city model: ~6,000 vehicles end of 2026 → ~15,000 end of 2027. 30-city model: ~10,000 end of 2026 → ~28,000-30,000 end of 2027. 50-city model: ~13,000-14,000 end of 2026 → ~40,000 end of 2027
Larry Goldberg projects 300,000-500,000 vehicles by 2030, vs Morgan Stanley's 30,000
Estimated 10-15 staging sites with 10-20 vehicles each = roughly 100-300 Model Y robotaxis currently staged

utilization.fact: Cybercab expected to be used approximately 50 to 60 hours per week in autonomous operation, versus roughly 10 to 11 hours per week for a personally driven vehicle
Cybercab unit economics modeled at 30% vehicle utilization (7.2 hours per day in service)
Cybercab targets 18-20 hours per day asset utilization versus a private car's 95% idle time
1,800 robotaxi forcing function defined as: 1,800 units × 200 miles/day × $1/mile net margin. At 50% utilization, the effective threshold would require ~3,600 vehicles

avg_empty_distance.fact: Cybercab unit economics modeled with 40% of miles being empty 'deadhead' miles

base_fare.fact: Current Austin robotaxi pricing is $3 base fare and $1.40 per mile, up from the earlier $1 base fare and $1 per mile

fare_per_mile.fact: Current Austin robotaxi pricing is $3 base fare and $1.40 per mile, up from the earlier $1 base fare and $1 per mile
Current ride-share market rate is $2-$4 per mile. Tesla pricing still well below this

price_per_kwh.fact: Cybercab unit economics modeled with charging at $0.16/kWh (earlier model) and $0.35/kWh (updated fleet builder model)
Tesla Supercharger cost estimate: ~$0.17/kWh vs Electrify America ~$0.50/kWh (~3x differential)

insurance_cost.fact: Cybercab unit economics modeled with insurance at $3,600/year

cleaning_cost.fact: Fleet builder model uses cleaning cost of $1,700/year per vehicle

spot_monthly_cost.fact: Fleet builder model uses parking cost of $6,000/year per vehicle ($500/month)

vehicle_unit_cost.fact: Cybercab internal COGS estimate: ~$17,000–$18,000; consumer price: ~$25,000
Cybercab target price: under $30,000 per unit
Cybercab assumed purchase price: $30,000; $5,000 down payment in unit economics model
Waymo vehicle cost: $200,000+ (due to sensor load) vs Cybercab sub-$30,000

remote_ops_ratio.fact: Supervisor-to-robotaxi ratio discussed: currently possibly 3:1, target of 10:1 would allow fleet tripling. Going from 3:1 to 4:1 would allow a one-third fleet increase

infra_cost_per_vehicle.fact: Tesla has at least 210 service centers across the US, framed as ready-made robotaxi operations hubs
Average distance between adjacent Supercharger stations in the US: approximately 150 miles

kwh_per_mile.fact: Tesla Supercharger cost estimate: ~$0.17/kWh vs Electrify America ~$0.50/kWh (~3x differential)

new_vehicles_per_year.fact: Robotaxi fleet expected to roughly double every month
